results.possibleIdentity: Serena Friedman is a physician and viticulturist who was active in Newark, New Jersey, during the late 1960s and early 1970s. She graduated from Weequahic High School in Newark in 1964 and subsequently attended Rutgers University-Newark for her undergraduate studies. Following her time at Rutgers, she enrolled at the New Jersey Medical School in Newark, where she was a medical student in the mid-1970s. Her academic and professional background involved technical fields such as biochemistry, biology, and organic chemistry. During this period, she likely registered as a technical volunteer with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that matched artists with engineers and scientists. Professionally, she established a medical practice in the Los Angeles area, specifically in West Hills, California. Later in her career, she and her husband, Michael Drucker, transitioned into the wine industry, founding and operating the Four Sisters Ranch Vineyards and Winery in Paso Robles, California.
